A living record of Sonoran Desert biodiversity.

An ever-growing record of the remarkable diversity of life across the Sonoran Desert. Built from thousands of field observations over more than a decade, the Compendium connects species, habitats, and ecological interactions into a freely accessible resource for naturalists, educators, and the endlessly curious.

An ever-growing record of the remarkable diversity of life across the Sonoran Desert. Built from thousands of field observations over more than a decade, the Compendium connects species, habitats, and ecological interactions into a freely accessible resource for naturalists, educators, and the endlessly curious.

Every species is part of a larger story. This project documents who eats whom, who pollinates whom, and countless other ecological relationships—helping transform isolated observations into connected knowledge.

Wildlife and natural history from the Sonoran Desert and beyond.
A growing collection of wildlife and natural history photography that celebrates the beauty, behavior, and hidden details of the living world.

An open reference for identifying North America's small mammals.

An open reference for identifying and understanding North America's small mammals. Combining detailed identification resources with structured data, WhiskerWiki helps researchers, wildlife professionals, and curious naturalists navigate one of the continent's most overlooked groups of animals.

An intimate experiment in transforming an urban yard into a haven for native plants and animals.

What happens when a suburban yard is designed for wildlife instead of lawns? For more than a decade, we've been transforming our Phoenix yard into native habitat while carefully documenting the return of the plants, insects, birds, and other wildlife that follow.

Oversteger Studios is a nonprofit collaboration between Rick Overson and Laura Steger focused on natural history outreach, desert ecology, scientific photography, and community-engaged data projects.

We build tools and stories that help people see and understand the living world, especially in arid ecosystems.

## Contact Us

**Email:** [info@overstegerstudios.org](mailto:info@overstegerstudios.org)

**Location:** Sonoran Desert, Arizona

We welcome collaborations, data-sharing, and feedback from fellow ecologists, photographers, educators, and curious people of all kinds.

Building knowledge, tools, and stories that deepen our understanding of biodiversity.
Through photography, artwork, open data, software, and long-term field projects, we create resources that inspire curiosity and make the natural world more accessible.
